subject: Public Health
title: Why Clean Water Matters
description: A 60-second lesson on Why Clean Water Matters.

hook.text: Dirty water kills more children than war, malaria, and HIV combined every single year.

buildup.text: Contaminated water carries bacteria, viruses, and parasites that cause diarrhea, cholera, and typhoid.

discovery.text: Diarrheal disease alone kills over 500,000 children under five annually—almost all from unsafe water.

twist.text: Clean water is not just about drinking—cooking, bathing, and food production all require safe water.

climax.text: Access to clean water reduces child mortality by up to 21% in the most affected communities.

punchline.text: Clean water is the cheapest life-saving medicine on Earth.

quiz.question: How many children under five die from diarrheal disease annually?
